title: Diagnose local Python 3.12 thread bootstrap failure in Schauwerk runtime
goal: Diagnose and remove the heim-pc-local Python 3.12 runtime condition that makes a fresh thread fail with RuntimeError: can't start new thread and can therefore break the normal Schauwerk REST doctor path, without weakening REST authorization checks or changing provider credentials.

approval.note: Observed during T001. System Python 3.10 and an isolated Python 3.11 runtime could create threads and complete the REST doctor; the project Python 3.12 environment could not create one thread. Diagnose host/interpreter/runtime state before changing anything. Do not rotate Miro credentials or weaken doctor checks.

acceptance.assertion: The Python 3.12 thread-start failure is reproduced or explicitly disproven on a fresh current Schauwerk main with process, cgroup, memory and interpreter evidence.
The root cause is identified at interpreter, virtualenv, libc, cgroup, process-resource or host-runtime level without guessing from the REST provider response.
The normal project runtime can start required worker threads and `schauwerk miro rest doctor --require-write --json` no longer fails locally before network I/O.
The fix does not bypass live REST authorization, reuse MCP OAuth or relax boards:write requirements.

metadata.source_finding: During T001, the project uv-managed Python 3.12.12 environment raised RuntimeError: can't start new thread before the REST doctor could perform a trustworthy live check. System Python 3.10 could start a thread; an isolated Python 3.11 environment completed the doctor successfully. Host readback did not indicate an obvious PID or memory ceiling.
does_not_establish: permission to recreate credentials or weaken live authorization checks
metadata.outcome: Schauwerk PR #115 identified missing PT_GNU_STACK metadata in the uv-managed Python 3.12 runtime, repaired CPython 3.12.12 to an explicit non-executable RW stack without weakening W^X, added a fail-closed output-only inspector and repair path, and restored direct/project thread creation plus the normal live Miro REST doctor.

validated_acceptance.reproduce: PASS: Fresh direct uv CPython 3.12.12 and the existing Schauwerk virtualenv both reproduced RuntimeError: can't start new thread; system Python 3.10 passed; RLIMIT_NPROC, cgroup PID usage, stack limit and available memory did not indicate resource exhaustion.
validated_acceptance.root-cause: PASS: strace showed the thread-stack mprotect requesting READ|WRITE|EXEC and failing with EPERM; readelf showed uv Python 3.12.11/3.12.12 lacked PT_GNU_STACK while the working 3.11 build declared GNU_STACK RW. After the digest-bound ELF repair, mprotect used READ|WRITE and clone3 succeeded.
validated_acceptance.normal-path: PASS: Direct CPython 3.12.12 thread creation, the existing Schauwerk virtualenv thread creation and the normal project command uv run schauwerk miro rest doctor --require-write --json all pass on merged main.
validated_acceptance.no-security-regression: PASS: Host W^X remained enforced; the repair declares PT_GNU_STACK RW only, requires no root, changes no credential or Miro scope, preserves the separate REST credential, and the live doctor still requires and proves boards:write with scopes boards:read and boards:write.

verification.local_validation: make validate: 782 passed; lint, compile and registry validation pass
verification.github_pr_checks: validate 3.11/3.12 and all observed CodeQL checks pass
